steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The text asks for the earliest example of spectroscopy data in the table. Two instruments collected spectroscopy data: VISIR at the European Southern Observatory (2006) and TEXES at the Gemini Observatory (2007 and 2019). Two other instruments—T-ReCS (Gemini) and LWS (W.M. Keck)—collected infrared imaging data, not spectroscopy, so they are irrelevant regardless of their dates.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: Among the spectroscopy sources, VISIR at the European Southern Observatory obtained data in 2006, one year before TEXES's earliest observation (2007). Choice A (TEXES, 2007) is a later spectroscopy entry. Choice B (T-ReCS, 2007) collected imaging, not spectroscopy. Choice D (LWS, 2003) is the earliest entry overall but also collected imaging, not spectroscopy. Therefore, VISIR at the European Southern Observatory (2006) is the earliest spectroscopy source. That confirms C is correct.
